revision: v2 — corrected to vendor-tier pattern per CTB doctrine
architecture_note: field_monitor.* IS the vendor staging layer (same tier as vendor.people, vendor.blog, vendor.ct). Sub-hub Middle layer reads FROM field_monitor tables. No new vendor tables needed. The Cloudflare Workers write to field_monitor.*; the bridge module in hubs/ reads from it.
transformation_statement: LinkedIn profile field values stored in field_monitor.field_state (CONST from vendor tier) → Talent movement signals via EVENT_TALENTFLOW_MOVE routed through movement_engine (VAR in sub-hub canonical tables)

ctb_compliance.vendor_staging: field_monitor.* — external tool writes here (Cloudflare Workers). Analogous to vendor.people, vendor.blog.
ctb_compliance.hub_middle_layer: hubs/people-intelligence/imo/middle/movement_engine/ — bridge reads from vendor tier, fires events into existing movement engine. Business logic only.
ctb_compliance.hub_scripts: hubs/people-intelligence/scripts/ — seed scripts (support surface, not CTB branch). Operational tooling.

data_flow.tier_1_vendor: field_monitor.url_registry → field_monitor.field_state → field_monitor.check_log (Cloudflare Workers own this tier — they fetch, parse, detect changes, write results)
data_flow.bridge: linkedin_monitor.py in hubs/people-intelligence/imo/middle/movement_engine/ reads field_monitor.check_log WHERE changed=true AND domain='www.linkedin.com', converts to DetectedEvent, fires into MovementEngine.detect_event()
data_flow.sub_hub_canonical: Movement engine processes event → writes to people.person_movement_history (canonical output)

task_sequence.title: DB Agent — Seed field_monitor.url_registry with 100 LinkedIn URLs
task_sequence.description: SQL script: SELECT 100 linkedin_url FROM people.people_master WHERE linkedin_url IS NOT NULL AND linkedin_url LIKE '%linkedin.com%' LIMIT 100. Normalize to domain='www.linkedin.com' + path='/in/{slug}'. INSERT INTO field_monitor.url_registry with check_interval_minutes=1440, is_active=true. Idempotent via ON CONFLICT (domain, path) DO NOTHING.
task_sequence.acceptance: 100 rows in url_registry with domain='www.linkedin.com'. SQL idempotent on re-run.

task_sequence.title: DB Agent — Seed field_monitor.field_state for title + company per URL
task_sequence.description: For each url_id seeded in TF-001: INSERT 2 field_state rows (field_name='current_title', field_name='current_company'). Status='ACTIVE', promotion_status='DRAFT', current_value=NULL. Idempotent via ON CONFLICT (url_id, field_name) DO NOTHING.
task_sequence.acceptance: 200 rows in field_state (2 per URL). All linked to linkedin.com url_registry entries.

task_sequence.title: Builder — Parser KV seed script for LinkedIn HTML extraction
task_sequence.description: Python script to populate Cloudflare KV (PARSER_KV namespace) with parser function bodies for 'www.linkedin.com::current_title' and 'www.linkedin.com::current_company'. These are the regex/selector functions that the parser-registry Cloudflare Worker executes against fetched LinkedIn HTML. Script uses wrangler CLI to write KV entries.
task_sequence.acceptance: KV entries created. Parser functions validated against sample LinkedIn public profile HTML.

task_sequence.title: Builder — linkedin_monitor.py (Middle layer bridge module)
task_sequence.description: Bridge module at hubs/people-intelligence/imo/middle/movement_engine/linkedin_monitor.py. Reads FROM vendor tier (field_monitor.check_log) WHERE changed=true AND the url_id maps to a www.linkedin.com entry. For each detected change: (1) look up the person_unique_id by reverse-mapping the linkedin_url path back to people.people_master, (2) create a DetectedEvent with event_type=EVENT_TALENTFLOW_MOVE and metadata containing old_value, new_value, field_name, (3) call MovementEngine.detect_event(). Kill switch: KILL_TALENT_FLOW_MONITOR env var. Sample gate: only processes urls seeded (100 max from TF-001).
task_sequence.acceptance: Module imports clean. Correctly reads from field_monitor.check_log (vendor tier). Correctly maps changes to EVENT_TALENTFLOW_MOVE. Kill switch halts execution. No logic in ingress or egress — all in Middle.

task_sequence.title: Doc — Update pipeline.md with Talent Flow Monitor section
task_sequence.description: Add 'Talent Flow — LinkedIn Monitor' section to pipeline.md. Document: vendor tier (field_monitor.* tables), bridge flow (check_log → DetectedEvent → movement_engine), kill switches, sample gate, data flow diagram showing vendor tier → bridge → sub-hub canonical.
task_sequence.acceptance: Pipeline doc shows vendor-tier pattern. No stale references.

task_sequence.title: Auditor — Compliance check
task_sequence.description: 14-rule audit. Key checks: AUD-003 (CTB — all new files in declared hub Middle layer or scripts support surface), AUD-004 (Transformation Law — field_monitor vendor CONST → movement_engine VAR traceable), AUD-005 (IMO — bridge reads vendor in Middle, no logic in I or O), AUD-008 (scope — no files outside allowed_paths).
